# Figure description: Картограмма_геофизической_изученности.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Общие_данные_для_всех_листов/Картограмма_геофизической_изученности.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **index/cartogram map** (картограмма) showing the geophysical survey coverage of a study area. Specifically, it is a **survey coverage diagram for aeromagnetic surveys** (аэромагнитная съёмка) at 1:1,000,000 scale, illustrating which sub-areas have been covered by aeromagnetic surveys at various scales. It functions as a **metadata map** — documenting the spatial extent and resolution of previously conducted geophysical work rather than showing geophysical data values themselves.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a multi-sheet area in **northern Kazakhstan**, corresponding to map sheets from the Soviet topographic/geological grid system. The sheets visible include:

- **XXXIII, XXXIV, XXXV** (upper row)
- **II, III, IV, V** (middle rows)
- **VII, VIII, IX** (lower-middle row)
- **XIV, XV** (bottom row)

These correspond to sheets within the **M-42 and N-42** 1:200,000 series referenced in the report context. The overall layout is shown in the **sheet index diagram** (Схема расположения листов) at the bottom left, which confirms the spatial arrangement of all named sheets.

The stamp visible at upper right reads **Казахская ССР Республиканский геологический фонд, Инв. № 36572**, confirming this is from **Report 36572** held in the Republican Geological Fund of the Kazakh SSR.

---

## Key Content

The map uses **different line styles** (shown in the legend) to indicate aeromagnetic survey coverage at **five different scales**:

| Line Style | Scale |
|---|---|
| Solid lines with close hatching | 1:500,000 |
| Dashed lines (medium) | 1:200,000 |
| Dash-dot lines | 1:100,000 |
| Widely spaced dashes | 1:50,000 |
| Fine dashed lines | 1:25,000 |

Additionally, **diagonal hatched polygons** indicate **отдельные профили** (individual/isolated profiles) — areas covered by single survey lines rather than full areal coverage.

**Numbered contours/polygons** are scattered across the map (e.g., numbers visible include: 82, 114, 38, 30, 251, 82, 601, 112, 121, 56, 43, 82, 112, 622, 600, 414, 556, 601, 43, 146, 87, 121, 632, 569, 112, 67, 27, 585, 416, 112, 704, 466, 121, 131, 104). These are **номер контура по каталогу** — catalogue reference numbers identifying each surveyed polygon in a geophysical survey registry.

The coverage is **irregular and patchy**, with denser/more detailed survey work concentrated in the central and upper portions of the study area (sheets III, IV, XXXIII, XXXIV), while southern sheets (XIV, XV) show sparser coverage. Several areas show **multiple overlapping surveys at different scales**, indicating progressive refinement of coverage over time.

The **sheet index** at lower left shows the geometric arrangement of all 14+ named map sheets, confirming the overall study area is roughly **L-shaped or cross-shaped** in plan view.

---

## Text and Labels

**Title (full):**
> КАРТОГРАММА ГЕОФИЗИЧЕСКОЙ ИЗУЧЕННОСТИ РАЙОНА РАБОТ

*(Drawing No. 23 on 6 sheets. Sheet 1.)*

> Республиканский геологический фонд
> Инв. № 36572

> К отчёту Западно-Тенизской партии ЦГПЭ за 1978–84 г.г.

*(To the report of the West-Teniz Party [CGPE] for 1978–84)*

- масштаб 1:500 000
- — " — 1:200 000
- — " — 1:100 000

- масштаб 1:25 000
- отдельные профили *(individual profiles)*

**Sheet labels on map:** XXXIII, XXXIV, XXXV, II, III, IV, V, VII, VIII, IX, XIV, XV

**Sheet index label:**
> СХЕМА РАСПОЛОЖЕНИЯ ЛИСТОВ *(Sheet location diagram)*

---

## Research Value

This cartogram tells a geologist or research assistant **which portions of the study area have been covered by aeromagnetic surveys, at what resolution, and under what survey campaign**, enabling assessment of data completeness and identification of gaps requiring new acquisition. It documents that the **West-Teniz geophysical party conducted surveys between 1978 and 1984** at scales ranging from 1:500,000 down to 1:25,000, with the most detailed work concentrated in the central sheets. For mineral potential assessment, this figure is essential for understanding **where subsurface magnetic data are reliable enough to support structural interpretation** (e.g., mapping basement depth, fault systems, or intrusive bodies relevant to ore deposit targeting) versus where coverage is insufficient.
